shared_properties: Prisoner head shape, torso width, tunic palette, and chain accent stay aligned with the fall base sprite.
Each frame still fits a strict 16x24px player footprint for stable collision tuning.
Outline weight stays at 1px so runtime mirroring remains crisp on either wall side.
differences: Sprite sheet is 32x24px with two brace frames that push hand and boot contact into the wall edge.
Torso leans 2px toward the contact side to communicate active braking rather than free-fall drift.
Animation rate slows to 6 FPS so the pose reads as controlled friction against the wall.

context.usage: Brace pose used when friction input engages a wall and the descent speed drops.

shape.silhouette: Compressed player pose with one hand and one boot pushing into a wall.
shape.collision_read: Contact limbs stay inside the 16x24px frame while still reading as wall pressure.

acceptance_criteria: Sprite sheet size is exactly 32x24px with two 16x24px frames.
The contact hand and boot reach the wall side of the frame without breaking the outer outline.
Runtime mirroring for the opposite wall keeps the silhouette readable with no asymmetrical noise on the face side.

reference_images.label: slip_down_player_reference
reference_images.purpose: Grounds prisoner proportions, cloth folds, and animation posing for the active sprite.

reference_images.label: slip_down_moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds dungeon lighting, palette temperature, and surrounding tile contrast for the active sprite.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: SpriteSheetBundle with a TextureAtlasLayout and fixed-step animation timer.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load through AssetServer during the Loading state and bind it to the player state machine after the fall loop is available.
runtime_notes: Use Bevy 0.13 with ImagePlugin::default_nearest() so every asset stays pixel-perfect at 4x upscale.
Drive gameplay motion in FixedUpdate at 60 FPS and keep art timing on deterministic timers.
Swap into this atlas when friction input is active and wall contact is confirmed by gameplay code.
